mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: psychosis, restless nights, splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, pulmonary arterial hypertension, decreased patellar reflex, lower limb muscle weakness, hemangiomatosis, sinus tachycardia

corneal clouding (not explicitly stated but implied by foveal and macular hyperpigmentation), mental retardation, coarse facies, dysostosis multiplex, short stature, cardiac involvement, liver enlargement, hernia, joint stiffness, hearing loss
angiokeratomas, hypohydrosis, renal failure, proteinuria, left ventricular hypertrophy, arrhythmias, ischemic attacks, neuropathy, anhidrosis, paresthesia

skeletal deformities including thoracic kyphosis, waddling gait, joint laxity, respiratory distress, dental anomalies, short stature, corneal opacification, conductive hearing loss, delayed motor milestones

progressive neurological deterioration with psychiatric symptoms, coarsening of facial features, growth deficiency, bone changes, heart problems, liver issues, testicular atrophy, sleep apnea, seizures, urinary tract infection susceptibility

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: Krabbe disease, atypical, due to saposin A deficiency
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: demyelinative disorder causing spastic paraplegia or quadriplegia, irritability, feeding difficulties, constipation, increased deep tendon reflexes, optic nerve damage leading to vision loss, seizures, poor head control, weak cry

muscular dystrophy characterized by progressive muscle wasting, especially affecting proximal limbs, macroglossia, hepatomegaly, hypoglycemia, cardiomyopathy, respiratory compromise, myoclonus, tremors, and intellectual disability

disproportionate tall stature, thin extremities, scoliosis, chest deformation, flat feet, lens subluxation, ectopia lentis, mitral valve prolapse, dilated aortic root, pectus excavatum, osteopenia, easy bruising, varicose veins, and joint hypermobility

joint hypermobility, chronic widespread pain, easy bruises, excessive scarring, gastrointestinal complications such as intussusception, diverticulitis, and malabsorption, vascular fragility resulting in ruptured blood vessels, and recurrent infections due to compromised immune system function

epilepsy, cognitive impairments, benign tumors called tubers that can develop anywhere in the brain and other organs, shaggy hair, uneven tooth enamel, noncancerous skin lesions known as ash leaf spots, fibromas under the nails, and retinal hamartomas
